title: blazewrit harness — Test step structured_output (RED tests + coverage/profile/migration-validate)
description: OUTPUT schema for the Test step. Test writes FAILING (RED) tests that verify Spec ACs; in Bug Fix it reproduces; in Performance it profiles/measures; in Migration it validates. TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation (M1 = SHAPE; the model literally cannot emit a wrong shape/enum/missing-required field). Truth-bearing fields (RED-confirmed counts, profile baselines, migration dry-run, AC traceability, declared next step) are closed by SIBLING M2 validator_contracts listed in x-validator-contract annotations and in the step manifest — grammar enforces SHAPE only. INPUT CONTRACT: exactly one of (Spec | Decide) — modeled as the `input` meta field, a oneOf discriminated by `input_kind` so grammar enforces exactly-one-of. §5: every cross-step ref (based_on, bug_ref) is a Postgres RowRef, never a file path. R23: every measured count is a CountClaim. M3: every tool-dependent fact (RED confirmation, firebat scan, perf baseline, migration dry-run) is a Measured|Omitted discriminated union. R13: `unverified` is a KEEP-polarity floor flag (the legacy test.md L40 'No [UNVERIFIED] remain' rule is DELETED — Verify is the single gate).

input.$comment: Input contract: exactly one of (Spec | Decide). Modeled as a oneOf discriminated by input_kind so grammar enforces exactly-one-of (R1/M1). The actual upstream rows are RowRefs in based_on; this meta records WHICH single input drove the step and its row.
input.description: INPUT CONTRACT (required-one-of). Exactly ONE upstream input drives Test: a Spec output (flows that have a Spec stage — Feature etc.) OR a Decide output (Spec-less chains — Bug Fix reproduce, Bug Fix Unreproducible hypothesis, Migration validate, Test-flow Decide(Plan)). Grammar enforces exactly one branch via the input_kind discriminant; per-branch `required` forces the matching row ref. x-validator-contract (M2): input_kind branch MUST match the orchestrator's chain (reviewer verifies flow chain).

spec_ref.description: RowRef to the driving Spec step_runs row (acceptance_criteria / tasks / code_architecture).

decide_ref.description: RowRef to the driving Decide step_runs row (option_selection / decision_record) for Spec-less chains.

tests_added.$comment: Core output. tests_added[{file_path, test_name, ac_ref, status:RED}]. status is a const enum (RED only) — grammar forbids any other value. Each item carries its RED-confirmation evidence (M3 degradable: the test run / firebat scan can fail/timeout/be absent), a provenance source_tool (R26 floor), and the R13 unverified KEEP-polarity flag.
tests_added.description: The RED tests this step wrote. Each entry is a single failing test traced to the AC (or reproduction/coverage target) it verifies. status is fixed to 'RED' (Test writes RED; Implement turns GREEN). ac_ref is the within-Spec AC identifier (e.g. 'AC-001') the test verifies — it is a SPEC-INTERNAL id, not a Postgres row (the Spec row is in based_on.spec_ref). M2 (test-reviewer/AC traceability): every Spec AC has at least one tests_added entry whose ac_ref names it; reverse, every ac_ref resolves to a real AC in the spec_ref row.

file_path.description: Path to the test file written (a real on-disk READ/WRITE path — code under git, NOT a §5 workflow-artifact RowRef; tests are versioned source files per DECISIONS §5 'code + spec/ADR = file (git)').

test_name.description: The test case identifier (function/describe-it name) that fails RED.

ac_ref.description: The acceptance-criterion identifier this test verifies (Spec-internal id, e.g. 'AC-001'). For non-Spec flows (reproduce/coverage) this names the reproduction or coverage target the test traces to. M2 (R-traceability): must resolve to a real AC/target in the input row.

status.description: Fixed to 'RED' — Test produces only failing tests. Grammar forbids any other value; Implement (GREEN) owns the transition.

red_confirmation.$comment: M3 DEGRADE — confirming RED requires RUNNING the test; that run can fail/timeout/be unavailable (firebat absent). Absence is a first-class branch (Measured|Omitted), NOT a null or placeholder. Measured.value carries the observed failing outcome.
red_confirmation.description: Evidence that running this test actually failed (RED confirmed). M3 Measured|Omitted discriminated union on `status`: 'measured' = the test was executed and observed failing (value carries the runner outcome); 'omitted' = the run could not be performed (e.g. test runner/firebat tool_failed/timeout/unavailable — R22 first-class absence). x-validator-contract (M2/R20-adjacent): orchestrator re-executes the test and asserts it fails (a self-asserted 'it's RED' is not truth).

failed.description: true = the test was observed to FAIL (RED). A RED test that does NOT fail is a contract violation (M2: re-execute and confirm).

command.description: Exact command run to confirm RED (e.g. 'bun test path/to/file').

raw_stdout_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the captured runner stdout. M2 re-hashes and re-derives the failing outcome.

source_tool.description: PROVENANCE (R26 floor, required). The tool/runner that produced this test entry's RED evidence (e.g. 'bun', 'firebat'). A test entry without a source_tool is unsourced and rejected.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor, KEEP polarity. NO 'no unverified remain' forbid (legacy test.md L40 DELETED). Verify is the single gate.
unverified.description: R13 KEEP-polarity floor flag. true = a claim about this test (e.g. it covers the AC's behavior, or a training-data-based assertion) was not verified by Test itself and MUST propagate downstream to Verify. Never forbidden true at this intermediate step.

tests_written_count.$comment: R23 count TYPE — 'N tests written' is a measured count, NEVER a bare integer.
tests_written_count.description: R23 CountClaim. The number of tests written, asserted as MEASURED (counted from the test runner / file scan). Bare integer forbidden — the model must emit the command + sha256 of its stdout. M2: re-parse source.raw_stdout, assert value derivable. Equals tests_added.length when consistent (cross-check is M2, not grammar).

red_confirmed_count.$comment: R23 count TYPE — 'N RED confirmed' is a measured count from re-running the tests.
red_confirmed_count.description: R23 CountClaim. The number of tests that were RUN and confirmed failing (RED). Measured count → CountClaim (command + raw_stdout_sha256). M2: re-execute the suite, re-derive the failing count from raw_stdout, assert value. Self-asserted 'N RED' is the exact R23 hallucination this forbids.

reproduction.$comment: Bug Fix flow only. {test_path, bug_ref}. bug_ref is a §5 RowRef (the Decide/bug-source row), NOT a file path.
reproduction.description: OPTIONAL — Bug Fix flow. The test that reproduces the bug (RED; Implement's fix turns it GREEN). Present only on Bug Fix chains. test_path is a real on-disk test file path (git-versioned code); bug_ref is a §5 RowRef to the upstream row describing the bug (Decide decision_record / Investigate). M2 (test-reviewer): the reproduction test actually reproduces the bug.

test_path.description: On-disk path of the reproduction test file (git-versioned code, not a workflow RowRef).

bug_ref.description: §5 RowRef to the upstream row that defines the bug (Decide/Investigate step_runs row). NEVER a file path.

profile.$comment: Performance flow only. {metric, baseline, target}. baseline is MEASURED (CountClaim) when available, but profiling tools are degradable → baseline is a Measured(CountClaim)|Omitted union (R22/M3). target is the design goal, not a measurement.
profile.description: OPTIONAL — Performance flow. The profiling measurement: which metric, its measured baseline, and the target. Present only on Performance chains. baseline is M3-degradable (perf tooling can be ABSENT/FAIL/timeout — R22 first-class absence), and when measured it is a CountClaim (a measured number, not a bare integer — R23). target is the goal the benchmark asserts against (an M2/R20 verify_probe-style threshold, not itself a measurement).

baseline.$comment: M3 + R23. Measured branch carries a CountClaim as its value (measured number with command+sha256); Omitted branch when the perf tool is absent/failed/timeout.
baseline.description: The measured baseline for `metric`. M3 Measured|Omitted on `status`: 'measured' = baseline obtained, value is an R23 CountClaim (the measured number + the command/raw_stdout that produced it); 'omitted' = perf tool absent/failed/timeout (R22 first-class absence). NOT nullable, NOT a placeholder. x-validator-contract (M2): re-parse the CountClaim source and re-derive the baseline number.

target.$comment: R20 — a target is a re-runnable assertion (command + expected_result), not a measurement.
target.description: The performance target the benchmark must eventually meet, expressed as an R20 VerifyProbe (command + expected_result). SHAPE only; M2 re-executes the probe against the implementation (Verify), not Test. Test writes the RED benchmark; it does NOT pass yet.

migration_validation.$comment: Migration flow only. {script_path, dry_run_result}. dry-run executes the migration tool → degradable (Measured|Omitted, R22/M3). script_path is git-versioned code, not a RowRef.
migration_validation.description: OPTIONAL — Migration flow. Validation of the migration script (reversibility / data-integrity / rollback). Present only on Migration chains. script_path is the on-disk migration script (git-versioned). dry_run_result is M3-degradable: running a dry-run can fail/timeout or the tool can be absent — absence is a first-class Omitted branch, not a null/placeholder. x-validator-contract (M2): re-execute the dry-run and confirm the recorded outcome.

script_path.description: On-disk path of the migration script validated (git-versioned code, not a workflow RowRef).

dry_run_result.$comment: M3 DEGRADE — the dry-run outcome. Measured branch carries the executed result + provenance; Omitted when the dry-run could not run.
dry_run_result.description: Outcome of the migration dry-run. M3 Measured|Omitted on `status`: 'measured' = dry-run executed (value carries outcome + the command that ran); 'omitted' = dry-run tool absent/failed/timeout (R22). x-validator-contract (M2): re-execute the dry-run, re-hash its stdout, assert the recorded outcome.

outcome.description: Dry-run verdict: did the migration apply cleanly and reverse cleanly?

command.description: Exact dry-run command executed (e.g. 'bun run migrate --dry-run').

raw_stdout_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the dry-run stdout. M2 re-hashes and re-derives outcome.

reversible.description: Whether the dry-run confirmed the migration is reversible / rollback-safe.

firebat_scan.$comment: agents/test.md L22 'Run firebat scan after writing test code'. firebat is MCP-backed → degradable (Measured|Omitted, R22/M3). Optional: only present when the step runs the scan.
firebat_scan.description: OPTIONAL — result of the firebat scan run after writing test code (agent rule). firebat is MCP-backed and degradable: M3 Measured|Omitted on `status` — 'measured' = scan ran (value carries findings count as an R23 CountClaim); 'omitted' = firebat unavailable/failed/timeout (R22 first-class absence). NOT nullable, NOT a placeholder.

findings_count.description: R23 CountClaim — number of firebat findings (measured count, not a bare integer).

based_on.$comment: §5 — cross-step refs are Postgres RowRefs, never file paths. Test requires its driving input ref (spec_ref XOR decide_ref, mirrored in `input`) and may carry optional enrichment refs (investigate_ref for edge-case priority, ground_ref for volatile baseline).
based_on.description: §5 BASED-ON bundle. RowRefs (Postgres step_runs rows) for the upstream steps Test consumed — NEVER file paths. The driving input (spec_ref or decide_ref) mirrors the `input` discriminant. Optional enrichment: investigate_ref (risk_surface for edge-case priority), ground_ref (volatile_state pass/fail baseline). x-validator-contract (M2): the present spec_ref/decide_ref matches `input.input_kind` and the orchestrator's chain.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor at step level, KEEP polarity. Aggregate flag that any tests_added[].unverified is true (or a step-level training-data assertion is unverified). NO 'no unverified remain' forbid — Verify is the single gate.
unverified.description: R13 KEEP-polarity floor flag (step level). true = this Test output carries at least one unverified claim that MUST propagate to Verify. DESIGNED to persist (enforcement.md L41 authoritative); never forbidden true here. The legacy test.md L40 'No [UNVERIFIED] remain' self-validation rule is DELETED — Verify is the single gate that resolves unverified claims.

declared_next_step.$comment: R16 — Test may DECLARE its successor (normally 'implement', the GREEN partner), but the orchestrator INJECTS the authoritative expected_next_step. Advisory only; chain check is M2 (folded into R27).
declared_next_step.description: R16 DECLARED-NEXT-STEP (advisory). Test names its expected successor (normally Implement, the GREEN partner in the RED-GREEN loop). This does NOT free-fill the authoritative value — the orchestrator injects expected_next_step. x-validator-contract (M2): assert declared_next_step == orchestrator's expected_next_step (chain divergence catch, R16 folded into R27).
